title: Is the salary worth the hell

cons: antiquated technology - lots of my work was on programming and maintaining plcs from the early 90's on call 24 7 - you will get called to the field in the middle of the night on weekends on holidays and while you're on a date you will not get compensated for these calls no mileage reimbursement - i drove over 13 000 miles for this company without being reimbursed unappreciated work - as an electrical field engineer specifically your job will essentially be a glorified electrical technician no matter how many 10pm nights you put in the technical innovations you come up with or the the millions of dollars of revenue you generate you will always be out ranked by the project managers and the business team sad but true

title: Rating and Ranking Cyclical and dying industry not recommended long term

cons: long term - you are simply an employee number at the end of the day the closer you get to retirement your ranking will drop and it will be convenient to let you go before reaching retirement worrying about your employment status every few years due to the industry if they over extend in oil exploration they apply the same layoff standard to all sides of the business no matter how profitable your team is highly recommend you avoid this corporation if you have a family and a good heart that truly cares about people moved all customer service jobs to buenos aires - does not care about employing usa - only cares about pennies on the dollar no matter where they can get it look at shell and chevron before exxonmobil as they allow their employees to interview for other jobs in the company before laying them off if you are looking for a sales position they are moving away from outside sales to support customers to save more
